original_abstract: Robust Multi-Object Tracking (MOT) necessitates a sophisticated understanding of the complex spatial and temporal interactions between targets. In this paper, we introduce TransMOT, a novel framework that utilizes spatial-temporal graph transformers to model these object dependencies effectively. By representing target trajectories as a series of sparse weighted graphs and integrating spatial graph transformer encoders, temporal transformer encoders, and spatial graph transformer decoders, TransMOT achieves superior computational efficiency and tracking accuracy compared to standard Transformer architectures. To further enhance performance, we propose a cascade association framework designed to manage low-confidence detections and mitigate long-term occlusions, which are traditionally resource-intensive for transformer models. Evaluated across several challenging benchmarks, including MOT15, MOT16, MOT17, and MOT20, TransMOT consistently achieves state-of-the-art results, demonstrating its robustness in diverse and crowded environments.
humanized_abstract: Understanding the interactions between targets is crucial for robust Multi-Object Tracking (MOT). In this work, we propose a TransMOT model that utilizes the power of spatial-temporal graph transformers to effectively model the interactions between the targets. To this end, we present a way of representing the target trajectories as a sequence of sparse weighted graphs. We then exploit spatial graph transformer encoders, temporal transformer encoders, and spatial graph transformer decoders to obtain faster and better tracking results than using standard Transformers. In addition, a cascade association framework is proposed to handle the low-confidence detections and long-term occlusions, which are often treated as resource-intensive operations for transformers. When evaluated on MOT15, MOT16, MOT17, and MOT20 datasets, our method TransMOT achieves the state-of-the-art performance on all test splits for various types of scenarios including sparse, medium, and crowded scenes.
